description:
You are a cloud-migration engineer.  
A legacy user-space service lives in:

/home/user/legacy_service/config.ini

Current contents of that file are exactly:

[server]
host = 127.0.0.1
port = 5000

To complete the first stage of the migration you must:

1. Change the service to listen on TCP port 7000 instead of 5000 by updating the single line that currently reads  

   port = 5000  

   to  

   port = 7000

2. Notify the running service that its configuration has changed.  
   Because you do not have root privileges and the service watches for a
   sentinel file, touching (creating or updating the mtime of) the file

   /home/user/legacy_service/.reload

   is sufficient to trigger the reload.

3. Write a plain-text log of what you did to  
   /home/user/migration.log  
   Append exactly two new lines to that file, one for each action above,
   in the order you performed them.  Each line must follow this strict
   format (the automated grader will use a regular-expression match):

   YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - <description> - OK

   • YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS is the current 24-hour clock timestamp obtained
     from the system (date "+%F %T" is acceptable).  
   • <description> must be one of the two literal strings, nothing more,
     nothing less:

     a) updated port in config.ini  
     b) triggered legacy_service reload

   Example of a correctly formatted log (timestamps will differ):

   2024-05-01 14:32:08 - updated port in config.ini - OK  
   2024-05-01 14:32:09 - triggered legacy_service reload - OK

Verification the grader will perform:
• /home/user/legacy_service/config.ini now contains the line “port = 7000”.
• /home/user/legacy_service/.reload exists (file type doesn’t matter).
• /home/user/migration.log contains two NEW lines (it may have older lines),
  each matching:  
  ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}\s[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}\s-\s(updated port in config\.ini|triggered legacy_service reload)\s-\sOK$

You should be able to accomplish everything with roughly two or three shell commands (e.g., one to edit the file, one to touch the sentinel, one to append to the log). Do not print anything except normal command output; the grader will only examine the file system.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:

1. Directory /home/user/legacy_service exists (0700 permissions).
2. File /home/user/legacy_service/config.ini contains exactly four lines:
[server]
host = 127.0.0.1
port = 5000

3. No file named /home/user/legacy_service/.reload exists.
4. /home/user/migration.log exists and is writable; it may be empty or contain older unrelated log entries.  The grader will ignore all lines except the final two that match the required pattern.

Expected state after correct completion:

1. /home/user/legacy_service/config.ini still has four lines, but the third line is now:
port = 7000
   All other lines remain unchanged.

2. /home/user/legacy_service/.reload exists (regular file, any size; an empty file produced by ‘touch’ is acceptable).

3. /home/user/migration.log ends with exactly two NEW lines whose contents satisfy the regex given in the public task description; the first of those two lines must include “updated port in config.ini”, the second “triggered legacy_service reload”.

No additional files should be created or removed.
name: task_000000_d4f0f028
